equations.raw: pi(H_i)^dag = Hi_32^dag
equations.context: H_i: q=i, q*=conjugate(i)=-i in the quaternion *-algebra. pi(-i) should be pi(H_{-i}) = -pi(H_i)

equations.raw: So pi(b*) = pi(b)^dag means Hi_32^dag = -Hi_32 (anti-Hermitian in matrix sense)
equations.context: pi(-i) should be pi(H_{-i}) = -pi(H_i) pi(H_i)^dag = Hi_32^dag

equations.raw: Let's check: is Hi_32 anti-Hermitian? (i.e., H_i^dag = -H_i)
equations.context: print(f"  (Should be 0 if anti-Hermitian, which means q*=-q for H_i)")

equations.raw: pi(lambda, q, m) = L(lambda, q) @ X @ R(m)
equations.context: The LEFT part diag(alpha, alpha_bar, q_2x2) IS a *-homomorphism. But the RIGHT part diag(1, m^dag) uses m^dag already, so:

equations.raw: pi(lambda*, q*, m*) = L(conj(lambda), q_bar, m^dag) @ X @ R(m^dag)
equations.context: But the RIGHT part diag(1, m^dag) uses m^dag already, so: pi(lambda, q, m) = L(lambda, q) @ X @ R(m)

equations.raw: = L^*(lambda, q) @ X @ R^*(m)
equations.context: pi(lambda, q, m) = L(lambda, q) @ X @ R(m) pi(lambda*, q*, m*) = L(conj(lambda), q_bar, m^dag) @ X @ R(m^dag)

equations.raw: # For L: lambda -> diag(lambda, ...), so L(conj(lambda)) = conj(L(lambda))
equations.context: = L^*(lambda, q) @ X @ R^*(m) where L^* and R^* are the representations of the *-algebra.

equations.raw: = diag(alpha_bar, alpha, q_2x2^T.conj())
equations.context: when lambda is in the (0,0) position. But q -> diag(alpha, alpha_bar, q_2x2), and q* -> diag(conj(alpha), conj(alpha_bar), q_2x2^dag)

equations.raw: # Let me just check NUMERICALLY whether pi(b*) = pi(b)^dag.
equations.context: = diag(alpha_bar, alpha, q_2x2^T.conj()) Hmm, this is getting complicated.

equations.raw: C: lambda* = conj(lambda). C_Im: (i)* = -i. C_Re_proj: (projector)* = projector.
equations.context: For each A_F generator, compute pi(b^*) and compare with pi(b)^dag. The *-operation on A_F:

equations.raw: H: q* = q_bar (quaternion conjugate). H_i: i*=-i, H_j: j*=-j, H_k: k*=-k, H_1: 1*=1.
equations.context: The *-operation on A_F: C: lambda* = conj(lambda). C_Im: (i)* = -i. C_Re_proj: (projector)* = projector.

equations.raw: M_3: m* = m^dag. E_{ab}_Re: (E_{ab})* = E_{ba}. E_{ab}_Im: (iE_{ab})* = -iE_{ba}.
equations.context: C: lambda* = conj(lambda). C_Im: (i)* = -i. C_Re_proj: (projector)* = projector. H: q* = q_bar (quaternion conjugate). H_i: i*=-i, H_j: j*=-j, H_k: k*=-k, H_1: 1*=1.

equations.raw: C_Im: lambda=i -> lambda*=-i
equations.context: AF_star_names = []

equations.raw: H_i*: q=i, q*=-i -> alpha=-i, alpha_bar=i, q_2x2=diag(-i, i)
equations.context: AF_star_names.append('C_Re_proj*')
AF_star_names.append('H_i*')

equations.raw: H_k*: q=k, q*=-k -> alpha=0, alpha_bar=0, q_2x2=[[0,-i],[-i,0]]
equations.context: AF_star_names.append('H_j*')

equations.raw: M_3 generators: m* = m^dag
equations.context: AF_star_names.append('H_1*')

equations.raw: E_{ab}_Re: m=E_{ab} -> m*=E_{ba} -> m^{*dag}=E_{ab} -> R=diag(1, E_{ab})
equations.context: M_3 generators: m* = m^dag

equations.raw: Wait: for M_3, pi(m) uses R=diag(1, m^dag). So pi(m*) uses R=diag(1, (m*)^dag)=diag(1, m).
equations.context: M_3 generators: m* = m^dag E_{ab}_Re: m=E_{ab} -> m*=E_{ba} -> m^{*dag}=E_{ab} -> R=diag(1, E_{ab})

equations.raw: So pi(m*) = build_bimodule_16(I, diag(1, m)).
equations.context: E_{ab}_Re: m=E_{ab} -> m*=E_{ba} -> m^{*dag}=E_{ab} -> R=diag(1, E_{ab}) Wait: for M_3, pi(m) uses R=diag(1, m^dag). So pi(m*) uses R=diag(1, (m*)^dag)=diag(1, m).

equations.raw: m* = m^dag for M_3(C): (val * E_{ab})^dag = conj(val) * E_{ba}
equations.context: m_elem = np.zeros((3, 3), dtype=complex) m_elem[a, b] = val

equations.raw: pi(m*): R = diag(1, (m*)^dag) = diag(1, m)  [since (m^dag)^dag = m]
equations.context: m* = m^dag for M_3(C): (val * E_{ab})^dag = conj(val) * E_{ba} m_star = m_elem.conj().T

equations.raw: Redo order-one with CORRECT o-map: o(b) = Xi @ conj(pi(b*)) @ Xi
equations.context: print(f"  Need to use J pi(b*) J^{{-1}} = Xi @ conj(pi(b*)) @ Xi instead.")

equations.raw: Variant 1: o(b) = Xi @ b^T @ Xi (what we've been using)
equations.context: return max_err

equations.raw: Variant 2: o(b) = Xi @ conj(b) @ Xi
equations.context: test_omap_variant(lambda b: Xi @ b.T @ Xi, "o(b) = Xi @ b^T @ Xi")

equations.raw: Variant 3: o(b) = Xi @ b^dag @ Xi
equations.context: test_omap_variant(lambda b: Xi @ np.conj(b) @ Xi, "o(b) = Xi @ conj(b) @ Xi")

equations.raw: Variant 4: o(b) = Xi @ b @ Xi (no conjugation at all)
equations.context: test_omap_variant(lambda b: Xi @ b.conj().T @ Xi, "o(b) = Xi @ b^dag @ Xi")

equations.raw: Variant 7: o(b) = conj(b) (just complex conjugate)
equations.context: test_omap_variant(lambda b: b.conj().T, "o(b) = b^dag (no Xi)")
